objective: Reviewed the health-gated explore-mode surface for `grove serve` against the approved plan and the seven task ACs, verifying code and gates independently.
key_changes: AC-1: determine_surface precedence force_standard > (force_explore|config) > health_probe; --explore/--standard clap flags; mcp.rs thin, core::explore executes.
AC-2: health_probe once pre-loop; healthy -> single explore tool, plain object schema (question required, no anyOf/oneOf); unhealthy -> 7-tool fallback + stderr note.
AC-3: call_explore_tool uses real OpenAiCompatClient; bad-args and all ExploreError paths return isError:true, not JSON-RPC errors; match exhaustive over ProviderDown|Client.
AC-4: ProviderDown -> isError with actionable endpoint/config/restart message; no crash or exit.
AC-5: Standard branch reuses tool_specs()/instructions()/call_tool() verbatim; existing tests only threaded the unavoidable &Surface::Standard arg, assertions unchanged.
AC-6: integration test explore_mode_unhealthy_provider_falls_back_to_standard_surface (port-1 -> 7 tools + 'falling back' stderr + initialize OK) present and passing.
AC-7: clippy --all-targets -D warnings clean; no 'fastcontext' string in cli/src or core/src.
Gates run independently: cargo test --release --locked = 178 pass (126 core + 32 cli-unit + 19 integration + 1 doc), 0 fail.
API contracts grove-verified: run_explore sig, ExploreError{ProviderDown,Client}, ExploreAnswer.text, ExploreConfig, OpenAiCompatClient::new, health_probe->HealthError all match call sites.
Advisory: missing-question isError and explore_tool_spec no-anyOf/oneOf shape lack direct unit guards (plan-review advisories); exercised only transitively.
Advisory: explore_instructions model/base_url interpolation untested; low risk.
